item.content:encoded:
i was expecting funny antics about falling into a trash can why am i crying

But no seriously, this is some top-notch writing. Good job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I did not expect to love this and relate to it so much. Quite a turn around from what the description made me think. Absolutely loved it, and I'm so glad to have read it!
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reading Description: is joke

Reading Interview Logs: very serious analysis on someone struck by the melancholy version of the "just throw me in the trash" blues, to quote It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia's Frank Reynolds

Well, what can I say but I like it. Something I can understand.

And I wish I could say more, but I forgot. Oh well, I think I said enough anyway

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fun fact, my original outline for this story had him going to the moonbase and ending up on an escape pod but I cut it because I couldn't get the tone right.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wonder what would have happened if he went to the moon base. Worst case scenario is probably the moon falling out of orbit.
trashman is a hero to us all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just wanted to say this article is what finally made me join the site after many years of reading SCPs. Which is to say, I think it's rather good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I came in with low expectations but this is quality, I'll go ahead and check out that album then. +1

Oh, and this "and vindictive sticker trying to dump me in the trash." at the time of reading (before I read the following logs) was absolutely hilarious. (there should probably be an "a" after the "and" though) :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Didn’t expect to relate as strongly as I did. Well done

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Still equally good as when i read it as a draft. im in a special thanks does that make me cool now?
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, didn't notice those typos and I've made the fixes you recommended. Glad you enjoyed the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll admit I was skeptical at the onset but this really is a well-done story. I found myself identifying with Parker a lot and the development of his depression feels very natural. Very easy +1 from me.

I did find a few grammatical errors; I'm not sure whether they were stylistic so I'll just put 'em under a block and let you sort it out.

+ show block
– hide block

Parker:Come on Avia, -> Parker: Come on Avia,
You could end up be Louie. -> You could end up being Louie.
I had that life, and I made the decision to out that in danger… -> Assuming out should be put?
What I'm learned is that I'm not really all that important. -> First I'm should be I've
it doesn't' care how I get there -> remove the second apostrophe
I might have missed a few, but those were some of the more glaring ones.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now that's what I call Boxing Day

item.title: Better
item.content:encoded:
As repeated attempts to prevent SCP-5544 have failed, and the resource cost of attempts at prevention are significantly greater than allowing its occurrence, no further attempts to contain SCP-5544 will be made. Every December 26th, at no later than 11:00PM, Researcher Zachary Parker will enter the main dumpster of Site 93 and remain within it for no less than 45 minutes. A Foundation security officer and Foundation psychologist will be posted nearby to help with the recovery of Researcher Parker from the dumpster. A mandatory psychological evaluation of Researcher Parker should begin immediately after, to assess his mental well being.

Like that, see?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought it was going to be comical, and I was very wrong. Pleasantly surprised here, +1!

item.title: "And leave you with nothing"
item.content:encoded:
“Cause anyone who ever had a brain
Wouldn't stand out in the rain
Or keep it up for very long
Just to prove somebody wrong”

- “The Blonde” by TV Girl

Thank you to the following awesome human beings for the crit I received to make this article something I’m really proud of:
Sevatar, Dr. Moned, Cybersqyd, Dr. Fullham, Scottyhoff, and AbsentmindedNihilist.

This went from idea to outline to draft to article very quickly. This was natural to write which probably says more about me than the article.

Not to reveal all my secrets, but obviously this is the most personal thing I’ve ever written for this site. This article’s purposes are twofold.

1. I wanted to talk about mental health (mine in particular) in a way that uses a metaphor to make it just a little more understandable. I hope that ended up working out. For anyone else out there who also has panic disorder, I hope you can find some kinship with the protagonist of this story.

2. I wanted to make a companion piece to the excellent album French Exit by T.V. Girl which is a possibly the best album of the last decade and deserves some more recognition. I don’t know if this is how Brad and the gang thought they’d inspire people, but if he ever ends up reading this I hope he enjoys all the little references I planted. There’s at least one reference to every song on that album buried in here somewhere.

Thanks for giving this article a read.

“But don't be fooled
'Cause it could have been you
Or him or anyone else”

- “Daughter of a Cop” by TV Girl
